reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully encodes all three parts of the informal claim: (1) `Irreducible p` for p = X^3 - 2 over ZMod 7, (2) `Irreducible q` for q = X^3 + 2 over ZMod 7, and (3) `Nonempty (Polynomial (ZMod 7) ⧸ span {p} ≃+* Polynomial (ZMod 7) ⧸ span {q})` which asserts the existence of a ring isomorphism between the quotient rings. Since ZMod 7 is the field F = ℤ/7ℤ, `Polynomial (ZMod 7)` is F[x], and `span {p}` (with `open Ideal`) is the principal ideal (p), so the quotient matches F[x]/(p(x)). Because p and q are irreducible, the quotient rings are indeed fields, and a `RingEquiv` between them is precisely a field isomorphism, matching "the fields are isomorphic". The parametrization of p and q via hypotheses `hp`/`hq` is equivalent to defining them directly. No hypothesis is added or dropped.
